summary: Matched a large set of video intro/cutscene functions across the vi module, especially OnEnter/setup routines for scene archives, cameras, fog, lights, models, player/stage initialization, and crowd/audio behavior. The PR also tightened cross-module prototypes in gr, pl, mn, and item headers so the new vi code could call existing systems with typed signatures instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS. Review feedback emphasized replacing raw offset access with pulled-out structs where possible, or using M2C_FIELD when the type is still unknown.
what_changed: Replaced multiple vi function stubs with matched implementations, including un_8031DE58_OnEnter, un_8031E444_OnEnter, un_8031EBBC_OnEnter, un_8031F07C_OnEnter, un_8031FD18_OnEnter, and several vi1202 crowd/audio helpers.

Added scene setup code that loads archives with lbArchive_LoadSymbols, creates HSD_GObj camera/fog/light/model objects, attaches CObj/JObj/LObj/Fog objects, registers GX links and procs, and requests/advances animations at static float start frames.

Added stage and player initialization sequences inside vi cutscene entry points, using Camera_80028B9C, mpColl_80041C78, Ground_801C0378, Stage_802251E8/Stage_8022532C, Item_80266FA8/Item_80266FCC, Player_InitAllPlayers, and related player setup calls.

Added vi0801 support for the Big Blue route sequence, including a local vi_GObj struct for gxlink fields, model setup from ViWait0801 data, camera pass rendering, and a typed grBigBlueRoute_8020DAB4(char*, float, int) call.
Added vi0601 support for the Corneria wait sequence, including grCorneria_801E1BF0 usage, child JObj scaling through fn_8031E800, model animation setup, and lighting setup.

Added vi1201v1 scene data typing via Vi1201SceneData, replacing raw offsets into un_80400258 with named fields such as player_spawn, scene1_file, scene1_symbol, scene2_file, scene2_symbol, scene3_file, and scene3_symbol.
Added vi1202 crowd reaction and audio functions such as un_80321D30, un_80321EBC, and un_8032233C, using CrowdConfig thresholds, fighter lookup/knockback helpers, and pl_8003FDA0.

Expanded headers from unknown signatures to typed prototypes, including grCorneria_801DDD4C(Vec3*), grCorneria_801E1BF0(void), grCorneria_801E2E50(enum_t), grCorneria_801E2EA0(void), mnCharSel_8025D1C4(int,int), mnCharSel_80264070(void), and pl_8003FDA0(int,int).
Moved item global declarations for it_804D6D40 into item headers and corrected its visible type in it_2725.c from an uncertain f32** comment block to f32*.

Adjusted smaller vi code for matching, such as vi0102_CameraCallback expanding vi_RunCamera behavior inline, vi0102_RunFrame becoming static, and vi0402 replacing M2C_FIELD byte writes with direct un_804D6F84 array indexing.
smart_moves: Introduced a real data-section struct, Vi1201SceneData, to name archive file/symbol pointers and player spawn data instead of continuing with opaque pointer arithmetic.
Converted many cross-module UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations into typed signatures before relying on them from vi code, reducing cast noise and making future vi/stage/player work safer.
Kept local-only callbacks static where evidence supported it, such as vi0102_RunFrame, vi0502_8031E328, vi0601_CameraCallback, and un_8031ED70.
Used existing HSD object fields such as gobj->hsd_obj and gobj->gxlink_prios instead of raw pointer writes where the object layout was known.

Preserved matching-sensitive expression shapes where needed, including explicit u64 shifts for audio mask constants, temporary locals such as tmp_cat, and control-flow rewrites in vi1202 boundary checks.
Centralized repeated cutscene setup patterns: audio/effects reset, archive loading, fog color capture, light object creation, camera animation, model animation, and OnFrame/proc registration.
decomp_lessons: For vi intro/cutscene files, OnEnter functions often follow a reusable recipe: reset audio/effects, load one or more scene archives, create fog/light/camera/model GObjs, register GX links/procs, then initialize stage/player/item systems if the sequence needs gameplay objects.

When a data blob is indexed repeatedly by fixed offsets, pulling out a partial struct can both improve readability and help reviewers accept the match; the Vi1201SceneData conversion is a concrete example.

Cross-module vi matches frequently force header cleanup outside vi. Matching a cutscene may require typed prototypes in gr, pl, mn, item, or fighter headers before the local function can be expressed cleanly.
Prefer typed object fields over raw offsets once the layout is known; examples include gobj->gxlink_prios and gobj->hsd_obj replacing manual writes/GET macros in some contexts.
Static data names and section comments are useful when exact symbols are still anonymous, but reviewers may push for structs or M2C_FIELD rather than ad hoc char-pointer casts.
If a PR regresses matches during large matching work, call it out and repair the regressions; the discussion contains an explicit note: "oops, will fix the broken/regressed matches".
naming_conventions: vi scene entry points use the existing address-derived un_*_OnEnter naming pattern, for example un_8031DE58_OnEnter, un_8031E444_OnEnter, un_8031EBBC_OnEnter, un_8031F07C_OnEnter, and un_8031FD18_OnEnter.
Per-frame callbacks keep RunFrame or OnFrame suffixes, such as vi0502_RunFrame, vi0601_RunFrame, vi_8031E6CC_OnFrame, vi_8031ED50_OnFrame, and un_8031F274_OnFrame.
Local callbacks may retain fn_* names when semantics are not yet known, for example fn_8031FAA8, fn_8031FCBC, fn_8031E800, and fn_8031EFE4.
Partial data structs are named after the owning module or scene, as with Vi1201SceneData and vi1202_UnkStruct.
Static headers such as vi0501.static.h and vi1202.static.h are used for file-local/static data and declarations needed by the matching source.
Headers retain address comments before prototypes and globals, for example /* 31DE58 */ void un_8031DE58_OnEnter(void* arg); and /* 4D6D40 */ extern f32* it_804D6D40;
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use partial structs for fixed-offset data blobs when enough fields are understood; review specifically preferred this over raw access in vi1201v1.
If a struct is not yet justified, use M2C_FIELD for unknown offset loads/stores rather than unsafe or unclear casts; review explicitly suggested "pull out into a struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD".
Small type and signature changes can be match-critical: pl_8003FDA0 changed from one to two int parameters even though its implementation ignores the second argument.
For constants too large or awkward for direct literals, expression form matters; examples include lbAudioAx_8002702C calls using (u64) 0x100 << 32, (u64) 8 << 32, and (u64) 0x20 << 48.
Introduce temporary variables to steer codegen, such as tmp_cat in un_803224DC and local char* mp before reading mpLib_80458868 offsets.
Use gotos or inverted conditions when required to preserve branch structure, as seen in vi1202's un_80322258 rewrite.

Casting callback signatures for GX link setup may be necessary when the local callback type is more specific than the API expects, for example casting vi0502_8031E328 and un_8031ED70 to void (*)(HSD_GObj*, int).

review_feedback.feedback: pull out into a struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD
review_feedback.context: Commented on un_8031FD18_OnEnter where raw access into input/data-style memory was being added.

review_feedback.feedback: pull out into struct (preferred) or use M2C_FIELD
review_feedback.context: Commented near un_8032233C and un_803224DC where mpLib_80458868 offset access was being changed.

review_feedback.context: Author acknowledged broken or regressed matches during the PR.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type Vi1201SceneData fields.
Check whether vi1202_UnkStruct and CrowdConfig were later fully documented or renamed.
Search for follow-up PRs touching mpLib_80458868 offset access in vi1202 after the struct/M2C_FIELD review comment.
Look for later cleanup of duplicated extern ftKb_Init_804D9570 in ftKb_Init.static.h from this diff excerpt.
Find PRs that convert remaining vi OnEnter un_* symbols to semantic names.
